title: [critic] Core projection identity: exact inner product & lift for score(roots(α,β)) is unspecified

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
The mathematical heart of the finite free Stam inequality (★) is a finite-$n$ analogue of the conjugate-variable identity
$$\rho_{X+Y}(X+Y)=\mathbb E[\rho_X(X)\mid X+Y]=\mathbb E[\rho_Y(Y)\mid X+Y].$$
We need an explicit inner product on $\mathbb R^n$ and a linear orthogonal projection / contraction $\Pi$ with
$$\operatorname{score}(\operatorname{roots}(\alpha,\beta))=\Pi(L_\alpha\operatorname{score}(\alpha))=\Pi(L_\beta\operatorname{score}(\beta))$$
for suitable lifts $L_\alpha,L_\beta$ from the $\alpha$- (resp. $\beta$-) coordinates to the $\mu=\operatorname{roots}(\alpha,\beta)$ coordinates.

**Why this is open, not routine.** The roots $\mu$ are a *nonlinear, $O(n)$-averaged* function of $(\alpha,\beta)$ via the MSS model $(p\boxplus_n q)(x)=\mathbb E_{Q\sim O(n)}\det(xI-(A+QBQ^\top))$, $A=\operatorname{diag}(\alpha),B=\operatorname{diag}(\beta)$. There is no a priori conditional-expectation structure as in the probabilistic (classical Stam) or operator-algebraic (Voiculescu, Invent. Math. 132, 1998) settings.

**Numerical status.** Only the zeroth-order shadow is confirmed: $\sum_i\operatorname{score}(\cdot)_i=0$ for each of $p,q,p\boxplus_n q$ (verified). The full $\Pi$ identity, its inner product, and whether $\Pi$ is an honest projection or merely a contraction are all UNVERIFIED and OPEN.

**Suggested routes.** (a) Resolvent form: $\operatorname{score}_i=-\frac{(p\boxplus_n q)''}{(p\boxplus_n q)'}\big|_{\mu_i}$, expand via diagonal resolvent entries of $A+QBQ^\top$ and seek a conditional-expectation over the $O(n)$ average. (b) Finite-free-cumulant coordinates (additive under $\boxplus_n$): the projection may become a literal coordinate projection.

Complements human issue q4-47; this issue isolates the *exact functional form* (inner product + lift) as the deliverable.
